rationale: The cited paper is used in the General Discussion as part of a specific theoretical contrast, not just a background list. Ramanathan and Dhar argue that their compatibility effects on basket composition are selective and therefore are not simply generalized affect transfer, citing Heilman, Nakamoto, and Rao (2002) as the relevant alternative account. This gives the citation a meaningful interpretive role in positioning the mechanism and contribution of the 2010 paper.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited work as an alternative promotion-effect explanation (generalized affect transfer) and then narrows that account by arguing its own effects are mechanism-specific and brand-directional. Thus, it refines the interpretation space around prior in-store promotion effects rather than merely mentioning them.
